subject: Astronomy & Space
title: 55 Cancri e: A Lava World
description: A quick, practical guide to 55 Cancri e: A Lava World.

hook.text: 55 Cancri e is a super-Earth orbiting so close to its star that its surface is likely an ocean of molten lava.

buildup.text: Its year is just 18 hours. Surface temperatures reach about 2,000°C—hot enough to melt most rocks.

discovery.text: It was one of the first super-Earths found, detected in 2004 orbiting a nearby Sun-like star just 41 light-years away.

twist.text: Early reports suggested it was made of diamond—carbon compressed under extreme conditions. That claim remains debated.

climax.text: JWST detected thermal emission from its surface in 2024, hinting at a possible atmosphere despite the extreme heat.

punchline.text: A year in 18 hours. Lava oceans and maybe diamonds under the crust.

quiz.question: What makes 55 Cancri e extreme even among exoplanets?
options: It orbits so close that its surface is molten lava with a year of just 18 hours
It is the largest known rocky planet at 50 Earth masses
It has the coldest surface temperature ever measured on a super-Earth
